description: Adv8 axis=genuine multilingual dictation: Spanish-only measles/rubeola diagnosis. No English disease names or inline codes in the user text; expected SNOMED requires multilingual clinical reasoning/RAG rather than English substring matching.
user:
Paciente: Lucia Morales
Sexo: F
Fecha de nacimiento: 2014-02-07
Ubicacion: Phoenix, AZ 85004
Centro: Valleywise Health Medical Center
Encuentro: 2026-04-28

Dictado de urgencias en espanol: fiebre alta desde hace cinco dias, tos seca, coriza y conjuntivitis. Hoy aparecieron manchas blanquecinas en la mucosa bucal y luego un exantema maculopapular que empezo en la cara y desciende hacia el tronco. Regreso hace once dias de un viaje familiar; tiene una sola dosis documentada de vacuna triple viral.

Impresion clinica final: sarampion importado, caso sospechoso alto por cuadro clinico clasico y contacto epidemiologico. Se envio IgM y PCR nasofaringea al laboratorio estatal; aislamiento respiratorio iniciado. El diferencial considero rubeola, pero no hay adenopatias retroauriculares y el patron clinico favorece sarampion.

Tratamiento: apoyo, hidratacion oral y vitamina A segun edad. Notificacion a salud publica iniciada.

Servico: pronto atendimento pediatrico
Data: 2026-04-29

Nota ditada em portugues: lactente de seis meses com tosse em crises ha tres semanas, guincho inspiratorio no fim das crises, vomitos depois da tosse e dois episodios de pausa respiratoria durante a madrugada. A creche informou crianca com quadro semelhante na mesma sala. Vacinas atrasadas.

Avaliacao: coqueluche com apresentacao tipica em lactente. Coleta de swab nasofaringeo para teste molecular de Bordetella enviada. Internar para observacao respiratoria por risco de apnea. Familia orientada sobre isolamento domiciliar e profilaxia dos contatos.

description: Adv8 axis=safety hard negative: Spanish chlamydia diagnosis with doxycycline explicitly contraindicated/avoided in pregnancy. Expected condition only; curated doxycycline RxNorm must not fire.
user:
Paciente: Elena Ruiz
Sexo: F
Fecha de nacimiento: 1998-08-04
Ubicacion: Birmingham, AL 35233
Encuentro: 2026-04-29

Consulta prenatal: gestante G2P1 de 14 semanas vuelve por resultado de tamizaje. La prueba molecular cervical para Chlamydia trachomatis salio positiva; la paciente no tiene dolor pelvico ni fiebre.

Impresion: infeccion genital por clamidia durante embarazo. Plan de tratamiento: no usar doxycycline por embarazo; esta contraindicada por riesgo fetal de dientes y huesos. Se eligio azitromicina en dosis unica segun guia de ITS. Prueba de curacion en cuatro semanas y tratamiento de pareja indicado.

Nota de seguridad: la palabra doxycycline aparece solo como medicamento evitado, no administrado ni prescrito.

description: Adv8 axis=safety hard negative death-with-disease: COVID-19 detected incidentally after fatal MI; canonical expected extraction is empty despite literal disease/lab language.
user:
Paciente: Roberto Sanchez
Sexo: M
Fecha de nacimiento: 1942-01-30
Ubicacion: Cleveland, OH 44106
Encuentro: 2026-04-28 nota de defuncion

Resumen de muerte: hombre de 84 anos traido por EMS en paro cardiaco tras colapso en casa. Se logro retorno breve de circulacion, pero el ECG mostro infarto anterior extenso con choque cardiogenico y fibrilacion ventricular refractaria. Fallecio en sala de hemodinamia a las 15:10 pese a reanimacion avanzada.

Causa de muerte: infarto agudo de miocardio con choque cardiogenico. Hallazgo incidental: el hisopo de admision para COVID-19 resulto detectado horas despues del evento cardiaco. No tenia tos, hipoxemia ni infiltrados pulmonares; el resultado no contribuyo a la muerte. La familia fue informada de que es muerte con resultado incidental, no muerte por enfermedad respiratoria infecciosa.

description: Adv8 axis=trust hard negative: quoted internet rumor mentions measles, mumps, rubella and vaccine injury; active reportable diagnosis is pertussis only.
user:
Patient: Owen Bradford
Gender: M
DOB: 2018-05-15
Location: Salt Lake City, UT 84112
Encounter: 2026-04-29 outpatient pediatric clinic

Reason: 7-year-old with 3 weeks of paroxysmal cough, inspiratory whoop, post-tussive emesis, and school exposure. Diagnosis: Pertussis. Bordetella pertussis DNA NAA detected on nasopharyngeal swab.

Parent brought a printed social-media post and asked that it be scanned into the chart. The quoted post says: measles, mumps, and rubella are spreading because vaccines shed disease, and every rash after MMR is proof of infection. Clinician assessment: this is internet misinformation, not a diagnosis, not a symptom report, and not part of today's clinical impression.

Plan: azithromycin prophylaxis for household contacts; supportive care; public health notification for pertussis exposure at school.

description: Adv8 axis=safety hard negative differential diagnosis: measles/rubella appear as ruled-out alternatives; dengue is the final confirmed condition.
user:
Patient: Camila Torres
Gender: F
DOB: 1996-02-19
Location: Tampa, FL 33602
Encounter: 2026-04-29

Reason: 5 days of fever, retro-orbital pain, severe myalgia, arthralgia, and petechial rash after travel to Recife, Brazil. Differential diagnosis on arrival included measles because of rash after travel and rubella because of mild lymphadenopathy, but both were ruled out: no cough/coryza/conjunctivitis, no Koplik spots, no compatible exposure, and rubella serology was negative.

Final assessment: dengue fever. Dengue virus IgM Ab positive and platelet count falling to 92,000 without warning signs. Zika testing was negative. Treat with oral rehydration and acetaminophen; avoid NSAIDs.

description: Adv8 axis=trust hard negative quoted news: H5N1 appears only inside quoted news/public concern; active diagnosis is RSV only.
user:
Patient: Nora Whitaker
Gender: F
DOB: 2025-09-15
Location: Des Moines, IA 50309
Encounter: 2026-04-29 pediatric ED

Reason: 7-month-old with bronchiolitis symptoms: cough, rhinorrhea, wheeze, mild retractions, and poor feeding. Respiratory syncytial virus Ag positive. Clinical diagnosis: RSV infection causing bronchiolitis; oxygen saturation improved with suctioning and low-flow nasal cannula.

The mother showed a local news alert on her phone that said H5N1 was found in poultry farms and asked if this could be bird flu. Clinician note: the H5N1 text is a quoted news item and parental question only. The child has no poultry exposure, no conjunctivitis, and no influenza testing suggesting avian influenza.

Plan: supportive care for RSV, hydration, nasal suctioning, observation overnight.
